answer:
Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* indeed features distinct poems titled "Insaan" ("Man" or "The Human Being") in Part 2 (chapter 14) and Part 3 (chapter 12), reflecting contrasting tones on human existence.[^1][^2]

The Part 2 poem adopts a pessimistic outlook, portraying humanity as uniquely tormented—an enigma hidden from itself amid a creation where everything else revels in its own nature. It opens with the "strange tyranny" of creation making Man a riddle concealed from his gaze, restless yet unable to grasp life's mystery.[^3] The poem culminates in Man's isolation: "There is no one to share the sorrow of Man; / How bitter is the existence of Man!"[^4] This emphasizes existential bitterness and alienation, contrasting nature's harmonious self-fulfillment (e.g., stars drunk on fate, the sun finding rest).[^5]

In contrast, the Part 3 poem presents a prouder, more dynamic vision of human potential, depicting Man not as passive but as a force of striving and transformation. Humanity's powers "are aflame with striving," yearning for vastness—like "a desert contracted to a point."[^6] It closes triumphantly: "If it so chooses, it can change the very form of the garden; / This being is wise, is perceptive, is potent."[^7] Here, Man transcends the garden's scenes, embodying wisdom and agency against a backdrop of submissive nature.[^8]

These differences highlight Iqbal's evolving emphasis: lamenting human anguish in Part 2, while affirming empowered self-realization in Part 3.

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/).
[^2]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 12, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/12-insaan/translation/).
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14, [p-d7ec8e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-d7ec8e).
[^4]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14, [p-62d764](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-62d764).
[^5]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14, [p-3ebd11–p-edc3e3](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-3ebd11).
[^6]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 12, [p-cb3c43–p-2697a9](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/12-insaan/translation/#p-cb3c43).
[^7]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 12, [p-587d8c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/12-insaan/translation/#p-587d8c).
[^8]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 12, [p-59c22a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/12-insaan/translation/#p-59c22a).
